tasks_completed.name: Distal-dendrite length sweep on t0024 DSGC
tasks_completed.key_finding: Primary DSI varies measurably on t0024 (0.545-0.774, slope -0.1259, p=0.038) — AR(2) noise unpins null firing from zero; non-monotonic shape falsifies both Dan2018 and Sivyer2013

tasks_completed.name: Distal-dendrite diameter sweep on t0024 DSGC
tasks_completed.key_finding: DSI-vs-diameter slope is flat (0.0041 per log2, p=0.88); diameter does not modulate DSI on t0024 while length does — new length/diameter asymmetry finding

tasks_created.name: Distal-dendrite length sweep on t0024 DSGC
tasks_created.reason: Retry the t0029 length sweep on the t0024 port where AR(2) noise keeps null firing non-zero, so DSI is measurable (source suggestion S-0027-01)

tasks_created.name: Distal-dendrite diameter sweep on t0024 DSGC
tasks_created.reason: Retry the t0030 diameter sweep on the t0024 port to discriminate Schachter2010 active amplification from passive filtering (source suggestion S-0027-03)

tasks_created.name: Rerun t0030 distal-diameter sweep on t0022 with halved null-GABA
tasks_created.reason: Unpin primary DSI on t0022 from 1.000 by reducing null-direction GABA from 12 nS to 6 nS (source suggestion S-0030-01, addresses the DSI ceiling flagged in t0029/t0030)

key_findings: AR(2) stochastic release rescue works: t0024 keeps null firing at 0.70-1.00 Hz vs t0022's pinned 0 Hz, making primary DSI a meaningful outcome on length and diameter sweeps
Length modulates DSI on t0024 (p=0.038) but diameter does not (p=0.88) — a clean asymmetry that cable theory explains via L/lambda scaling linearly in length but as 1/sqrt(d) in diameter

Primary DSI on t0034 is non-monotonic (0.545-0.774) and falsifies both Dan2018 monotonic growth and Sivyer2013 saturating plateau; vector-sum DSI shows clean monotonic decline (R^2=0.91) consistent with cable filtering past an optimal electrotonic length
Preferred-angle jumps (0 deg to 330 deg at L=1.5x, 0 deg to 30 deg at L=2.0x) fingerprint Schachter2010 local-spike-failure transitions superimposed on cable-filtering dominance

Diameter sweep on t0024 is completely flat (slope 0.0041 log2, p=0.88) — neither Schachter2010 active amplification (predicted positive slope) nor passive filtering (predicted negative slope) fits the data
13 new suggestions filed; zero-cost L/lambda collapse analysis (S-0035-01) can test the cable-theory asymmetry hypothesis on existing data with no new simulations
t0036 started at 21:57Z local to rerun t0030 diameter sweep with halved null-GABA (12 nS to 6 nS) to unpin DSI on t0022 and complete the four-cell comparison
